# Echo Persistence — Chapter I · Foundations

> The mechanism and the model. Why durability is one knob (throughput tracks how many durable records ride each fsync), the two axes a queue is placed on (loss window × whether state leaves the box), and the engine-neutral vocabulary both engines later share — Volume, LSN, snapshot, the OCC commit, the conditional-write fence. Three modules, each a sub-folder with a hub `index.md` plus three dives. Links are to the readable `.md` sources (each has a rendered `.html` sibling).

## Navigation

- **Up: course root** — [../llms.txt](../llms.txt) · the course landing/map — [../index.md](../index.md)
- **Prev:** Overview — [../overview/llms.txt](../overview/llms.txt) · **Next:** Chapter II · The local store — [../local-store/llms.txt](../local-store/llms.txt)

## Module 1 · The durability spectrum

> Durability is a position on a spectrum, set deliberately per queue. The module builds the spectrum as a tradeoff frontier, extracts the one knob (durable records per fsync) that moves a system along it, and separates the two axes a queue is placed on.

- **Module hub** — [durability-spectrum/index.md](durability-spectrum/index.md)
- **Dive 1.1 · Inherited Valkey durability** — what AOF's once-a-second flush actually promises; trigger a crash and watch the lost window appear — [durability-spectrum/inherited-valkey-durability.md](durability-spectrum/inherited-valkey-durability.md)
- **Dive 1.2 · The shootout and the one knob** — the mechanism with a records-per-fsync slider; the four engines fall out as positions, not a table — [durability-spectrum/the-shootout-and-the-knob.md](durability-spectrum/the-shootout-and-the-knob.md)
- **Dive 1.3 · Two axes, one decision** — loss window and replication are independent; drop a workload into the durability–replication plane — [durability-spectrum/two-axes.md](durability-spectrum/two-axes.md)

## Module 2 · Champ, the accept tier

> The first tier says yes fast. Champ holds the outbox in a persistent in-heap structure and writes a snapshot to disk every K records, so the accept path runs at heap speed while a single fsync covers a whole interval — K is the loss window and the throughput dial at once.

- **Module hub** — [champ-accept-tier/index.md](champ-accept-tier/index.md)
- **Dive 2.1 · The in-heap structure** — insert a key and watch the new version share every untouched node; structural sharing makes a snapshot nearly free — [champ-accept-tier/the-in-heap-structure.md](champ-accept-tier/the-in-heap-structure.md)
- **Dive 2.2 · The checkpoint dial** — K is the loss window; feed the gauge, watch the at-risk window fill and a snapshot fire, set K and see the rhythm move — [champ-accept-tier/the-checkpoint-dial.md](champ-accept-tier/the-checkpoint-dial.md)
- **Dive 2.3 · Recovery & replay** — a crash, then restore from the last snapshot and replay the intents after it; step the sequence — [champ-accept-tier/recovery-and-replay.md](champ-accept-tier/recovery-and-replay.md)

## Module 3 · Persistence concepts

> Before either engine, the words. Both engines speak the same five — Volume, LSN, snapshot, the OCC commit, the conditional-write fence. Learn them engine-neutral here and the two engines later are the same idea twice.

- **Module hub** — [persistence-concepts/index.md](persistence-concepts/index.md)
- **Dive 3.1 · Volume, LSN, snapshot** — the log as an immutable ribbon; scrub to any past LSN and read the snapshot you would see — [persistence-concepts/volume-lsn-snapshot.md](persistence-concepts/volume-lsn-snapshot.md)
- **Dive 3.2 · The OCC commit** — two writers, one head LSN; one appends, the other is told `{:conflict, head}` and retries. The local serialization — [persistence-concepts/the-occ-commit.md](persistence-concepts/the-occ-commit.md)
- **Dive 3.3 · The conditional-write fence** — two nodes write the same commit object; create-if-not-exists lets one through and rejects the other. The remote fence — [persistence-concepts/the-conditional-write-fence.md](persistence-concepts/the-conditional-write-fence.md)
